# Fixed issues moved from issues.org.

* FIXED #129 Runtime Value instance payload fields now own their data
  Both ~StructInstance.data~ and ~EnumInstance.data~ in
  ~src/self/interpreter.til~ are now declared as ~own mut data : U8 = 0~
  (owned storage) instead of the old ~ref mut data : U8 = NULL~ (borrowed).
  This makes the runtime instance wrappers explicitly own their payload
  buffers. Related runtime lifecycle cleanup continues under #127 and #109.

* FIXED #126 Auto-generated enum delete now recurses into payload-owned data
  ~generate_enum_delete_method~ in ~src/self/initer.til~ now:
  - iterates all variants and checks which is active via ~is_variant()~
  - calls ~PayloadType.delete(get_payload(self), false)~ for owning variants
  - filters out ~Dynamic~ and ~FuncPtr~ types (not directly owning)
  This was the dominant leak source identified in #109 ASAN investigation.
  The clone side (#128) is still open -- auto-generated clone does not
  deep-clone managed payloads.

* FIXED #111 TypeScope / ScopeFind ASAN use-after-free
  The original deterministic ASAN use-after-free in the self-hosted
  type-scope lookup path is fixed. Root cause: generated switch lowering
  took a payload pointer from a ~ScopeFind.Found(b)~ wrapper, then freed
  the wrapper before using ~b.struct_def~. Fix: added borrowed
  ~TypeScope.get_binding()~ helper and switched hot lookup paths to use it.
  ~make test_asan~ now gets well into ~bin/tests~ instead of crashing early.
  Remaining memory pressure (leak volume, not crashes) tracked under #109.

* FIXED #110 Stack allocation and memory leak roadmap
  Meta-issue organizing #111 and #109. With #111 fixed (original UAF gone)
  and #109 as the active leak-reduction tracker, this roadmap is absorbed.
  The remaining goal (switch ~make test~ to always use ~bin/til_asan~) is
  now noted in #109.

* FIXED #131 `mode` syntax: keyword vs special identifier/directive
  Made ~mode~ a normal identifier instead of a reserved keyword.
  The parser recognizes it only at the file-header position (first
  non-comment token). After that, ~mode~ is a regular identifier
  available for variable/field names.

* FIXED #99 Struct literals restricted for types with new()
  Types that define a ~new~ namespace method cannot use struct literal
  syntax ~T(...)~ outside their own namespace. The typer enforces this:
  ~infer_struct_constructor_fcall~ checks if the struct body has a
  namespace method named "new", and if so, whether the current scope is
  inside that struct's namespace (tracked via ~_current_type_name~ global
  set/restored in ~infer_decl_stmt~). Violations are reported as type
  errors directing the user to ~T.new(...)~.

  Prep commits removed trivial ~new~ methods (TypeScope), replaced raw
  ~Array(data=...)~ views in game examples with ~board_get_i64~/
  ~board_set_i64~ accessors, and replaced raw ~Vec(data=...)~ literals
  with ~Vec.new~.

* FIXED #121 Kwargs heterogeneous storage no longer relies on raw Map values
  The old kwargs lowering built:

  - ~Map.new("Str", Str.size(), "", MaxType.size())~

  and used the empty ~val_type~ as a heterogeneous raw-storage escape
  hatch. That stopped working once ~#120~ made empty explicit types
  illegal.

  What changed:
  - added a dedicated ~DynMap~ core container
  - ~DynMap~ is currently a Str-keyed heterogeneous map implemented over
    ~Map(Str, Tuple)~
  - each stored value is wrapped as a one-element ~Tuple~
  - ~DynMap.get(...)~ unwraps that tuple, so user code still uses:
    - ~opts.has("x")~
    - ~opts.get("prefix")~
  - kwargs parameters now parse and lower as ~DynMap~, not ~Map~
  - ~desugar_kwargs_calls~ now builds ~DynMap.new()~ plus
    ~DynMap.set(key, type_name, type_size, value)~
  - ~src/test/kwargs.til~ and its test-harness entry were restored

  Result:
  - kwargs no longer depend on the old empty-~val_type~ raw-map escape
    hatch
  - heterogeneous kwargs storage is explicit again and test-covered

* FIXED #120 Container constructors reject empty explicit types
  Landed after ~#118~ made pointer/ref container storage explicit under
  ~Dynamic~.

  What changed:
  - ~Vec.new("", ...)~ now panics with
    ~"Vec.new: elem_type required"~
  - ~Map.new(..., "", ...)~ now panics with
    ~"Map.new: val_type required"~
  - ~Dynamic.size()~ was added and is now used for true
    pointer/ref-like ~Dynamic~ container storage
  - container constructors still manually clone stored type-name strings
    as the temporary workaround tracked by ~#119~
  - obvious non-pointer container sites were converted to explicit real
    type names
  - pointer/ref container sites continue to use explicit ~Dynamic~
  - AST/fake-AST raw storage that is not ordinary typed construction was
    normalized to direct raw ~Vec~ struct literals instead of empty-type
    constructor calls

* FIXED #118 Container element typing for pointer/ref container uses
  Decision landed:
  - use ~Dynamic~ as the explicit container element/value type for
    pointer/ref storage

  What changed:
  - typed containers now treat ~"Dynamic"~ as the explicit raw-pointer
    path instead of trying to dispatch through ~dyn_fn("Dynamic", ...)~
  - ~Vec~ and ~Map~ use default/raw clone-delete behavior for
    ~Dynamic~ payload storage
  - handwritten C pointer/ref container sites were updated to use
    ~"Dynamic"~ instead of ~""~
    - ~Expr *~
    - ~Str *~
    - ~ffi_type *~
    - similar pointer/ref-like scratch payloads
  - added focused coverage in ~src/test/vecs.til~

  Result:
  - pointer/ref container storage is no longer the ambiguous empty-type
    case
  - the remaining follow-up is ~#120~: reject empty explicit container
    types once the remaining conversions are done

* FIXED #106 For-in loop counter should be stack-allocated after #50
  This fell out of the #50 stack-local rework; no extra code change was
  needed afterward.

  Re-checked in generated C from ~src/test/arrays.til~:

  - collection temp is a value local:
    ~Array _fc_Array_304;~
  - for-in index is a value local:
    ~U32 _fi_USize_304 = 0;~
  - range counter is a value local:
    ~U32 _rc_U32_492 = 0;~
  - loop variable is also a value local:
    ~U32 i = U32_clone(&_rc_U32_492);~

  So the old heap forms like ~U32 *_rc = malloc(sizeof(U32))~ are gone.

* FIXED #50 Stack allocation for all struct/enum types
  This was landed incrementally and then verified again after ~#104~ and
  ~#106~ were done.

  What changed:

  - builder stack-local tracking was made explicit
  - raw allocation locals were normalized under the ~Dynamic~ exception
  - ordinary non-~ref~, non-~Dynamic~ locals now use stack-backed value
    storage and pass by stable ~&x~ addresses
  - value globals now use the same model via ~static T x~
  - for-in temporaries and range counters also fall out as value locals

  Re-verified after the final steps:

  - generated C from ~src/test/arrays.til~ shows:
    - ~Array _fc_Array_304;~
    - ~U32 _fi_USize_304 = 0;~
    - ~U32 _rc_U32_492 = 0;~
  - ordinary locals/globals no longer use the old heap-wrapper model
  - ~systemd-run --user --scope -p MemoryMax=64G make test~ passes
    (~82/82~)

  Remaining ownership/leak work now belongs under ~#109~ / ~#110~, not as a
  separate open ~#50~ problem.

* FIXED #104 Module-level mutable globals
  Landed once #50 had made ordinary values stack-backed.

  What was changed:

  - top-level value declarations are now pre-registered in
    ~init_declarations~, so functions can reference module globals before
    the declaration appears later in the file
  - builder now emits module globals as value storage
    (~static T x~ / ~static void *x~), not heap pointers
  - global reads, writes, returns, ~ref~ borrows, and ~mut~ calls now use
    value-global semantics consistently (the same ~&x~ / direct assignment
    model as stack locals)
  - generated headers now declare real source-level top-level globals for
    handwritten C (~extern T x~ in ~modes.h~ / ~*_forward.h~)
  - generated root translation units now own those global definitions, so
    handwritten C modules can link directly against til-defined globals
  - compiler-generated lowering temporaries (~_t_*~) stay internal and are
    not leaked into the generated C ABI
  - bootstrap / debug / ASAN direct-C builds were aligned with the same
    root-unit ownership rule by compiling the root generated unit
    (~til.c~), not ~til.c~ + ~modes.c~ together
  - this was verified by moving simple typer counters like
    ~hoist_counter~, ~_va_counter~, and ~_kw_counter~ from C statics to
    til globals and rebuilding cleanly
  - globals tests were strengthened to cover forward references to globals
    declared later
  - the typer error fixture was updated so its first undefined-symbol case
    remains undefined under the new forward-reference behavior

  Validation:
  - ~make clean~
  - ~systemd-run --user --scope -p MemoryMax=64G make test~
  - result: ~82/82~ passed
  - direct C consumption of til globals now works through generated
    declarations in ~boot/modes.h~

  Remaining work:
  - namespace/static-field globals still need the same treatment
  - that is now part of ~#70~, not a remaining ~#104~ blocker

* FIXED #64 pure and pura are library modes, not runnable modes
  The old issue text was stale. Two different things had been conflated:

  - ~pure~ / ~pura~ were still being treated as runnable/interpretable in
    some paths, even though they are library-style modes like ~lib~ / ~liba~
  - normal ~returns Type~ was being read as a C ABI bug because generated
    library headers use pointer returns like ~I64 *~, but that is the
    documented qualifier behavior, not a builder regression

  What was changed:

  - Added a single ~Mode.is_library~ field and set it for ~lib~, ~liba~,
    ~pure~, and ~pura~
  - Removed the old helper split between ~mode_is_lib~ and
    ~mode_is_lib_output~
  - ~til run~ and ~til interpret~ now reject ~pure~ / ~pura~, the same as
    ~lib~ / ~liba~
  - Tests now cover ~pure_build~, ~pura_build~, and the forbidden
    ~pure/pura~ run/interpret paths

  Re-checked after the fix:

  - ~systemd-run --user --scope -p MemoryMax=64G make test~ passes
    (~79/79~)
  - ~bin/til build~ works for minimal ~mode pure~ and ~mode lib~ files
  - Generated ~I64 *~ for ~returns I64~ is expected per
    ~doc/qualifiers.org~
  - Generated plain ~I64~ for ~returns shallow I64~ is also correct

  So the original ~I64* instead of I64~ complaint was outdated. The real
  problem was that ~pure~ / ~pura~ were not being treated consistently as
  library modes.

* FIXED #112 Normalize local qualifier defaults and reject redundant syntax
  Landed in incremental steps:

  - clearer parser diagnostics for redundant default qualifiers:
    - explicit ~ref~ params
    - ~returns own Type~
    - local ~shallow~
  - redundant local ~own~ is now rejected for both inferred and typed locals
  - typed ~Dynamic~ locals use the normal local-default spelling too:
    ~x : Dynamic = ...~, not ~own x : Dynamic = ...~
  - builder hoisting was updated so typed locals fed by ~Dynamic~-returning
    calls like ~malloc~ and ~dyn_call_ret~ stay heap-backed when needed,
    even without explicit local ~own~
  - repo-owned locals were cleaned up to use the default spelling
  - ~doc/qualifiers.org~ now matches the implemented default-based model

  Resulting local forms:
  - ~x := expr~
  - ~mut x := expr~
  - ~ref x : Type = expr~
  - ~ref mut x : Type = expr~

  Rejected redundant/invalid forms:
  - ~own x := expr~
  - ~own mut x := expr~
  - ~own x : Type = expr~
  - ~own mut x : Type = expr~
  - local ~shallow~
  - ~returns own Type~

  Validation:
  - ~make clean~
  - ~systemd-run --user --scope -p MemoryMax=64G make test~
  - final result: ~82/82~ passed

* FIXED #69 Bundle tinyfiledialogs for mode gui
  Bundled tinyfiledialogs (zlib license) in lib/tinyfiledialogs/.
  Integration follows the raylib pattern: direct ext_func declarations
  in gui.til, linked via ~link("tinyfd")~. No ext.c wrappers.

  gui.til: ~tinyfd_saveFileDialog~ and ~tinyfd_openFileDialog~ declared
  as ext_func returning ~ref U8~. Added ~c_str_len~ ext_func (one-line
  wrapper in ext.c for strlen -- can't use "strlen" as ext_func name
  because generated C forward declaration conflicts with libc). Wrapper
  functions ~save_file_dialog~/~open_file_dialog~ convert ~ref U8~ to
  ~Str~ via ~Str(c_str, count, CAP_VIEW).clone()~.

  editor.til: Ctrl+S on new file opens save dialog, Ctrl+O opens file
  dialog. Ctrl detection checks both ctrl (341/345) and alt (342/346)
  key codes -- GLFW uses physical key positions, so xmodmap Ctrl-on-Alt
  reports as 342 not 341.

  builder.c: ~-Llib/tinyfiledialogs -ltinyfd~ linked for all programs.
  ~Str_lit~ uses ~strlen(s)~ for count instead of passed len (C compiler
  processes escape sequences in string literals, so strlen gives the
  correct count).

  interpreter.c: Added escape sequence processing for ~LiteralStr~
  evaluation (~\n~, ~\t~, ~\\~, ~\"~, ~\0~). Previously the interpreter
  used the raw AST count which included escape bytes (e.g. ~"\n"~ had
  count=2 instead of 1).

  Makefile: ~libtinyfd.a~ build target added (built from source like
  raylib, not committed).

* FIXED #105 Replace per-variant is_*/get_* with generic ext_funcs
  Replaced auto-generated ~is_Variant()~ and ~get_Variant()~ methods
  (N functions per enum) with two generic ext_funcs:
  ~is_variant(self, other)~ and ~get_payload(self)~.

  - ~is_variant~ compares tags: ~*(I32*)self == *(I32*)other~
  - ~get_payload~ returns pointer past tag: ~(U8*)self + sizeof(I64)~
  - ALL ~.get_X()~ and ~.is_X()~ replaced in .til files
  - Switch desugaring uses ~is_variant~ / ~get_payload~
  - Auto-generated eq/clone/to_str updated
  - ~is_Variant~ / ~get_Variant~ generation removed from initer.c
  - is/get C functions removed from builder.c
  - is/get dispatch handlers removed from dispatch.c
  - ~ScopeFind_get_Found~ replaced with ~(TypeBinding*)get_payload~
  - Bare variant refs emit ~&(EnumType){.tag=TAG}~ compound literals
    (no malloc). Return paths heap-allocate.
  - Builder: ref-to-FuncPtr DEREF fix, ref_locals tracking
  - Interpreter: FuncSig/EnumDef narrowing for ref decls
  - Clone scoping fix: payload ~ref _pN~ decl in ~then_body~

* FIXED #108 Unify interpreter and builder enum memory layout
  Changed EnumInstance from ~{ Str *enum_name, I32 tag, Value *payload }~
  to ~{ Str *enum_name, Expr *enum_def, U8 *data, I32 data_size }~.
  The ~data~ buffer uses the same flat layout as compiled C:
  ~{ I32 tag; payload... }~ at ENUM_PAYLOAD_OFFSET (8 bytes).

  Updated all interpreter enum operations (read_field, write_field,
  clone_value, free_value, values_equal, constructors, is_Variant,
  get_Variant, FFI arg passing) to use the flat buffer layout.

  This enables generic ~is_variant~ and ~get_payload~ ext_funcs to
  work in both interpreted and compiled modes.

* FIXED #107 Core methods return shallow where possible
  All frequently-called methods now return by value instead of
  heap-allocated pointers:
  - str.til: contains, starts_with, ends_with, is_empty (shallow Bool),
    find, rfind, to_i64 (shallow I64)
  - vec.til: len (shallow USize)
  - ast.til: Expr.child_count (shallow USize),
    enum_has_payloads (shallow Bool)
  - map.til: has (shallow Bool)
  - set.til: has (shallow Bool)
  - Auto-generated comparison methods (eq, lt, gt, neq, lte, gte)
    via GEN_CMP_FUNC return_is_shallow in initer.c
  - All C dereferences (*Str_eq, *Map_has, *Set_has, *Str_rfind,
    *enum_has_payloads) removed from src/c/ callers


* FIXED #77 til.til: import self-hosted modules, use ~til build~ in Makefile
  Goal: til.til imports self-hosted modules directly instead of link_c,
  and Makefile uses ~til build src/til.til~ instead of two-pass gcc.

** DONE til.til imports self-hosted modules
   til.til now does ~import("self/modes.til")~ which pulls in the full
   chain: modes -> initer -> parser -> lexer (+ core/ast auto-prepended).
   Real types (Expr, Mode, TypeScope, etc.) replace ext_struct stubs.

** DONE Bridge reduction: til_bridge.c removed
   All 15 bridge functions replaced with native til operations.
   src/c/til_bridge.c deleted. link_c directive removed from til.til.

** DONE Makefile: ~til build src/til.til~ replaces two-pass gcc
   ~bin/til_current~ is now built by ~til_boot build src/til.til~.
   No more gcc for the current compiler. Self-build is idempotent --
   generated C is identical (zero diff) between til_current and
   the self-built binary.

* FIXED #80 USize type alias for container sizes
  Introduced ~USize~ as the semantic container-size type and completed
  the flip from ~U64~ to ~U32~ across the self-hosted compiler,
  runtime, core containers, and examples.

  Landed work:
  - core container fields and APIs now use ~USize~ for counts, caps,
    element sizes, and indices
  - C/runtime/codegen paths were made alias-aware so pass 1, bootstrap
    regeneration, and pass 2 all agree on ~USize~
  - committed bootstrap updated to match the new ~USize := U32~ world
  - parser/AST size-count fields like ~nparam~, ~variadic_count~, and
    ~kwargs_count~ now use ~USize~
  - follow-up cleanup removed redundant conversion churn and added
    ~to_usize()~ for semantic size/index casts

  Special cases fixed during the migration:
  - ~Tuple~ stores runtime type sizes as concrete ~U32~ values while
    exposing them semantically as ~USize~
  - string sentinels ~CAP_LIT~ and ~CAP_VIEW~ now use ~USize~
  - ~make rescue~ was fixed to use rescue-local headers, so recovery
    builds the last commit's compiler instead of leaking live headers

  Validation:
  - full self-hosting flow works with regenerated bootstrap
  - ~make test_headless~ passes ~73/73~

* FIXED #100 Scalar Range: type-aware for-in for builtin integer types
  Specialized the for-in desugaring in typer.c for Range. When the
  iterable is ~Range.new(start, end)~ (from ~..~ syntax), the typer
  desugars directly to a while loop with inc/dec instead of going
  through Range.new/len/get:

  #+begin_src til
  // for i in start..end desugars to:
  {
      _end : T = end
      mut _cur : T = start
      if _cur.lte(_end) {
          while _cur.lt(_end) { i : T = _cur; _cur.inc(); body }
      } else {
          while _cur.gt(_end) { i : T = _cur; _cur.dec(); body }
      }
  }
  #+end_src

  Element type T inferred from operands: non-literal wins, both
  literals default to I64. Zero overhead -- no Range allocation,
  no dyn_fn, no FuncPtr dispatch.

  Prep commits: proc -> void func (delete, swap, push, set, add),
  distance method + IncFn/DistanceFn typedefs on all numeric types.

  Updated piano.til and music.til with full type audit: constants
  and loop vars use U64 where semantically non-negative.

  Generic case (Vec2, custom types) deferred to #94 (post).

* FIXED #79 Type aliases in FuncPtr typedef signatures
  Bodyless FuncDefs (FuncPtr typedefs like ~DistanceFn~, ~CloneFn~)
  were breaking early in the typer before reaching alias resolution.
  Added resolution for return type and param types in the early-exit
  path. ~DistanceFn := func(Dynamic, Dynamic) returns USize~ now
  correctly resolves ~USize~ to ~U64~ in generated C.

* REOPENED #79 Type aliases -- moved back to issues.org (FuncPtr forward decls don't resolve)

* FIXED #88 UFCS with ~own~ first arg: ~own p.delete()~
  Parser now marks ~is_own_arg~ on the FieldAccess callee for
  statement-level ~own expr.method()~. Typer propagates it to the
  UFCS-inserted first arg after desugaring. ~own p.delete()~ now
  correctly desugars to ~Point.delete(own p)~.

* FIXED #98 Allow funcs without return type
  Removed the "func must declare a return type" restriction from
  typer.c. Funcs can now be void (like procs) while keeping the
  purity guarantee (no side effects). Macros still require a return
  type.

* FIXED #89 Revert to monolithic compilation (remove per-module file splitting)
  Removed per-module .c/.h emission from builder.c (#74). All struct/enum
  defs, function declarations, and function bodies now go into a single .c
  with forward declarations inlined. The ~mode lib~ .h (~modes.h~) is still
  generated separately by ~build_header~.

  ~bootstrap/~ dropped from 70 files to 6: ~modes.c~, ~modes.h~,
  ~modes_forward.h~, ~til.c~, ~til.h~, ~til_forward.h~.

  ~self_diff~ was already removed in 5d365ff18. ~compile_lib~ and
  per-library scavenging remain (separate concern).

* REOPENED #79 Type aliases -- moved back to issues.org (FuncPtr forward decls don't resolve)
  (Callback := BinaryOp), alias in function params/returns, alias with
  containers, alias arithmetic/comparison, alias in struct fields,
  alias in struct with methods (simulates #80 container use case).

* FIXED #73 Replace #ifndef guards with #pragma once
  All src/c/ headers now use ~#pragma once~ instead of
  ~#ifndef/#define/#endif~ guards, matching bootstrap convention.

* FIXED #67 For-in range should coerce literal 0 to match the end type
  Added implicit numeric widening: smaller integer types are automatically
  promoted to larger ones when passed as function arguments. Safe widenings:
  U8→U16/U32/U64/I16/I32/I64, U16→U32/U64/I32/I64, U32→U64/I64,
  I16→I32/I64, I32→I64. No signed→unsigned, no narrowing.

  Changes:
  - typer.c: added can_implicit_widen() check in both arg validation loops
    (namespace functions and regular functions)
  - interpreter.c: added widen_numeric() and needs_widen() helpers to
    convert values at function call sites (both ident and expression args)
  - Tests: loops.til (for-in range with U32 count), types.til (explicit
    widening tests for U32→U64, U32→I64, U8→U64)

  ~for i in 0..count~ where count is U32 now works without manual conversion.

* FIXED #12 Self-hosting: eliminate ctil.c, use only til.til
  src/ctil.c deleted. til.til (src/til.til) is now the sole compiler
  entry point. The compiler binary is bin/til_bootstrap, built via
  two-pass gcc from bootstrap/ (see doc/self.org for build workflow).

  What was done:
  - til.til verified as functionally equivalent to ctil.c for all tests
  - Fixed til.til user_argv passing (added til_interpret_v bridge)
  - Fixed til.til exit code propagation (exit(result.to_i64()))
  - Fixed builder compile_c() for multiple link_c files (compile each
    individually instead of batch cc -c with -o)
  - Fixed forward.h conflict: skip -include forward.h for src/c/ files
    that have their own headers
  - Test suite simplified: dropped ci/cr (ctil interpret/run) modes,
    kept only interp/run. Test count halved from ~160 to 73.
  - Split compile vs run timing in test suite (compile_ms column)
  - Makefile restructured: single bin/til_bootstrap target, two-pass
    build (gcc → til_core regenerate bootstrap/ → gcc), removed all
    ctil targets (bin/ctil, ctil_core, ctil_asan, ctil_dbg)
  - Debug targets renamed: bin/til_bootstrap_asan, bin/til_bootstrap_dbg
  - bootstrap/til.c + bootstrap/til_*.c/h now tracked in repo
  - Str.split() moved from vec.til standalone to str.til namespace
  - doc/totals.csv migrated from old format (ci_ms,cr_ms,ti_ms,tr_ms)
    to new (interp_ms,compile_ms,run_ms)
  - README.org, CLAUDE.md, agents.md, doc/self.org updated

  #39 continues separately for bottom-up C module elimination.
  #77 filed for til.til to use import instead of link_c for ast.til.

* FIXED #76 Shallow Expr_clone corrupted struct body defaults
  Root cause: auto-generated Expr_clone used Vec_clone with elem_type=""
  which did a shallow memcpy of children. Inner Expr nodes shared their
  children Vec data buffers with originals. When hoist_fcall_args modified
  cloned struct default expressions, it corrupted the original struct body,
  causing subsequent constructors to reference stale temp variables.
  Fix: custom deep Expr.clone method in ast.til that loops children and
  clones each one individually.

* FIXED #71 Bootstrap cleanup: clean ast swap
  src/c/ast.c replaced by bootstrap/ast.c (generated from ast.til).
  src/c/ast.h stripped to thin wrapper including bootstrap/ast.h.
  Makefile: bootstrap/lexer.c removed from SRCS (ast subsumes lexer).
  ctil_core: lexer.til translation removed (ast.til imports it).
  ext.c: includes bootstrap/ast.h instead of bootstrap/lexer.h.
  Builder: scalar methods + helpers emitted as non-static.
  Builder: null guard in auto-generated _delete methods.

* FIXED #75 Value-based Expr children + ownership discipline
  Expr children changed from sizeof(Expr*) to sizeof(Expr).
  Expr_add_child takes ownership (Vec_push frees child).
  Expr_child returns borrowed ref into Vec buffer.
  Real TilType/Declaration/ExprData clone/delete implementations.
  All callers clone borrowed refs before passing to own parameters.
  Precomp registers namespace funcs for constant folding.

* FIXED #74 Builder: emit separate .c and .h per source module
  Each til source module gets its own .c (function bodies) and .h
  (struct definitions + function declarations). Auto-generated functions
  (clone/delete/eq/to_str/size, enum helpers) stay in the main file.

  Per-module .h: struct/enum definitions + function declarations.
  Shared ~_decls.h~: forward declarations (typedef struct X X; + enum tags).
  Per-module .h includes ~_decls.h~ + cross-module .h for inline field deps.
  Per-module .c includes its own .h.
  Main .c includes all per-module .h files (replaces inline struct typedefs).
  forward.h unchanged (full defs needed by link_c files via -include).

* FIXED #72 link_c files can't see til-defined types and functions
  Builder emits ~gen/c/forward.h~ with all struct definitions (topo-sorted),
  enum tag enums, and function declarations. link_c files get it via
  ~-include~ flag when compiled to .o separately.

  Key design: ext.h is untouched — forward.h is invisible to the ctil
  build (avoids conflicts with hand-written ast.h, same pattern as the
  old pre70 shim removal). ~compile_c~ compiles link_c .c to .o separately with
  ~-include forward.h~, then links the .o. ~ffi_init~ (interpreter path)
  also generates forward.h and uses ~-include~.

  ext_struct types (Point, Pair in ext_module.c) no longer need manual
  C typedef — forward.h provides them from the til declarations.

* FIXED #32 Keyword arguments (kwargs) capture
  Functions can capture unmatched named arguments as an implicit ~Map<Str, Dynamic>~
  using the ~opts: ...~ syntax (three bare dots, no type). Follows the variadic
  pattern: ~..Type~ for variadic (implicit Array), ~...~ for kwargs (implicit Map).

  Desugaring creates ~Map.new~ + ~Map.set~ calls before the function call, then
  passes the Map as a single argument. Values accessed via ~opts.has(key)~ and
  ~ref val : Type = opts.get(key)~.

  Changes: lexer.til (DotDotDot token), parser.c, ast.h, ast.c, typer.c
  (desugar_kwargs_calls), builder.c, initer.c.
  Test: src/test/kwargs.til

* FIXED #59 Bundle all dependencies: zero external packages needed
  ~git clone~ + ~make test~ just works on any Linux with a C compiler.
  No ~apt install~ needed.

  What was bundled:
  - ~lib/raylib/~ — raylib via git subtree (~30MB, built from source)
  - ~lib/libffi/~ — libffi built from source (configure + make)
  - ~lib/x11/include/~ — X11 headers for compiling raylib's GLFW backend

  Link-time dependencies eliminated:
  - ~-lGL~ removed: GLFW loads GL via ~dlopen("libGL.so.1")~ at runtime,
    raylib uses bundled ~glad.h~ for GL headers
  - ~-lX11~ removed: GLFW loads all X11 libs via ~dlopen~ at runtime.
    Raylib built with ~-DSUPPORT_CLIPBOARD_IMAGE=0~ to avoid the one
    function (~GetClipboardImage~) that called X11 directly
  - Generated GUI binaries depend only on libc/libm (~ldd~ shows no
    libGL, libX11, libraylib)

  Also fixed:
  - ~make clean~ now cleans raylib and libffi builds
  - libffi generated files (~Makefile~, ~doc/libffi.info~) gitignored
    so ~make clean~ doesn't leave unstaged changes

  To update raylib:
  ~git subtree pull --prefix=lib/raylib https://github.com/raysan5/raylib.git master --squash~

* FIXED #60 Trailing commas anywhere they make sense
  The parser already supported trailing commas in all comma-separated contexts
  due to the ~if (check(Comma)) advance(p)~ pattern inside
  ~while (!check(closing_delim))~ loops. No parser changes needed.

  Contexts supported: function call args, func/proc parameter declarations,
  enum variant declarations, struct constructors, method call args, map/set
  literals, FuncSig type params.

  Regression test: src/test/trailing.til

* FIXED #17 Global variable access
  :DEPENDENCIES_OF: #12 #13
  Variables declared in script-mode root scope (or any outer scope) were
  invisible at runtime inside function/proc bodies. The typer passed
  (TypeScope chains via parent pointers and finds the variable), but the
  interpreter crashed or errored.

  Reproduction: src/test/globals.til, src/test/globals_cli.til

  func case: "error: undefined variable 'x'" — clean error from NODE_IDENT
  NULL check. proc case: SEGFAULT — NODE_DECL move-semantics path had no
  NULL check on scope_get result.

** Root cause

   expr_uses_var() in typer.c returned 0 for NODE_FUNC_DEF, meaning the
   ASAP pass didn't see variable references inside nested function/proc
   bodies. When x was declared at top level and referenced only inside a
   proc body, ASAP thought x was unused and inserted I64.delete(x) right
   after the declaration. When the proc later ran and tried to access x,
   the value had already been freed/nulled.

   func case: I64.clone(x) inside the function body tried to clone x
   whose value was val_none → clean "undefined variable" error (from
   precomp, which also couldn't find x in its limited scope).

   proc case: I64.clone(x) dereferenced v.i64 which was NULL → SEGFAULT.
   The NODE_DECL move-semantics path also lacked a NULL check on
   scope_get result, which was a secondary crash path.

   Secondary issue: precomp created its own interpreter scope with only
   func/struct/enum defs (no variable decls), then tried to fold pure
   func calls. If the func accessed a global variable, precomp's
   eval_expr hit the error path and exit(1)d before the interpreter
   even ran.

** Fix

   Program-scope globals live for the program's lifetime — they should
   never be ASAP-deleted. The scavenger handles removing unused globals.

   1. Skip ASAP deletion for program-scope variables: added skip_delete
      flag to LocalInfo in typer.c. Variables with a declaration at program
      scope (is_program_scope && decl_idx >= 0) get skip_delete=1. The
      ownership-transfer check still runs for them (use-after-own errors),
      but no delete calls are inserted. Variables captured by nested
      functions also get skip_delete=1 (expr_used_in_nested_func check).
   2. Added NULL check in interpreter.c NODE_DECL move-semantics path.
   3. Fixed precomp: added func_uses_unknown_globals() to skip folding
      func calls whose body references variables not in the precomp scope.
      Also made track_literal() populate the precomp interpreter scope
      with known constant values (not just the known map).
   4. Fixed builder: top-level variable declarations are emitted as
      file-scope C globals (before function definitions) so they're
      accessible from emitted til functions/procs. This is necessary
      because til functions are emitted as separate C functions, and C
      doesn't support nested functions capturing locals — so top-level
      vars must be file-scope for functions to reference them. The
      values are then assigned inside main() where initialization runs.
      In cli mode, globals are initialized at the start of main()
      before the user's main body.
      Note: ref declarations are skipped from file-scope globals since
      they are pointer aliases (local to main), not owned values that
      other functions need to access.

   Tests: src/test/globals.til (script mode), src/test/globals_cli.til (cli mode).

   Explicit capture syntax split out as #54.

* FIXED #28 Aliases: implicit ref for comptime-immutable declarations
  When ~a := b~ and b is provably immutable, ~a~ becomes an auto-alias
  (implicit ref — pointer to same storage) instead of a clone. The
  compiler detects when aliasing is safe with no new syntax needed.

  Auto-alias conditions (all must hold):
  - Destination is not ~mut~ (immutable target)
  - RHS is a ~NODE_IDENT~ (variable reference)
  - Source is not ~mut~ (immutable source)
  - Source is not ~own~ (not an ownership transfer)
  - Source is not an explicit ~ref~ (unless it's itself an auto-alias)
  - Source is not a parameter (params are borrowed, aliasing them
    and returning would create dangling refs)

  Implementation:
  - typer.c: auto-alias detection sets ~is_ref=true~ and new ~is_alias=true~
    flag on both NODE_DECL and TypeBinding. Alias chaining supported:
    ~x := 42; y := x; z := y~ — all three share the same storage.
  - typer.c: returning an auto-alias auto-inserts clone (prevents
    use-after-free since alias shares storage with a local).
  - initer.h: added ~is_alias~ field to TypeBinding to distinguish
    auto-aliases from explicit ~ref~ declarations.
  - Interpreter and builder already handle ~is_ref~ correctly — no
    changes needed. Aliases use ~scope_set_borrowed~ (interpreter)
    and pointer assignment (builder), same as explicit refs.
  - ASAP lifetime extension already handles refs transitively via
    fixed-point propagation — works for alias chains too.

  Tests: src/test/aliases.til (17 tests covering I64/Str/Bool/struct
  aliases, alias chaining, deep chains, returned aliases, mut exclusion,
  struct field cloning, param cloning).

* FIXED #45 Zero compiler warnings everywhere
  Eliminated all 6 -Wno-* warning suppressions from the Makefile and
  builder.c. All generated C code now compiles clean with -Wall -Wextra
  -Werror and no suppressions.

  Codegen fixes in builder.c:
  - unused-but-set-variable / unused-variable: emit (void)varname; after
    every NODE_DECL that declares a variable (covers hoisted scalars,
    non-hoisted pointers, for-in loop variables, etc.)
  - incompatible-pointer-types: return boxing paths now use the function's
    declared return type (from current_fdef) instead of the expression's
    til_type, fixing I64*/I32* mismatches
  - return-type: bare return; in main() now emits return 0; (tracked via
    in_main_func flag set during main body emission)
  - unused-parameter: emit (void)paramname; for all function parameters
    (was previously only done for self)
  - pointer-sign: hoisted scalar variables from fcalls now use the callee's
    actual return type instead of the expression's til_type, fixing I64/U64
    signedness mismatches. Added shallow_local_types Map to track the actual
    C type of each hoisted local, used by emit_as_ptr for compound literals.
    Non-shallow fcall hoisted pattern uses explicit cast on _hp pointer.

* FIXED #47 Struct/enum redeclaration silently overwrites — now errors
  *Original bug:* ~init_declarations~ Pass 1 in initer.c unconditionally
  set ~b->struct_def = expr_child(stmt, 0)~, so a second struct/ext_struct
  with the same name replaced the first. When user code wrote
  ~I64 := ext_struct { ... }~, it overwrote the core I64 definition
  (losing eq, add, sub, etc.). Same pattern in typer.c.

  *Fix:* Added redeclaration checks in both initer.c and typer.c:
  - initer.c ~init_declarations~ Pass 1 (structs) and Pass 2 (enums):
    before ~tscope_set~, check if a binding with ~struct_def~ already
    exists. If so, emit error with both locations and skip.
  - typer.c struct/enum registration: before ~tscope_set~, check if
    existing binding has a different ~struct_def~ pointer. If so,
    emit type error with both locations.
  - ctil.c: now checks ~init_declarations~ return value and exits
    on errors (previously return value was ignored).

  *Files changed:*
  - ~src/c/initer.c~ — redeclaration checks for structs and enums
  - ~src/c/typer.c~ — redeclaration check (defense in depth)
  - ~src/ctil.c~ — check init_declarations return value
  - ~src/test/test_47.til~ — updated to test cross-namespace calls
    (without redeclaration)

* FIXED #53 Payload enum in struct field: codegen bug
  Compiled mode produced wrong values when a payload enum was stored as
  an inline struct field. Interpreter worked correctly.

  Root cause: varargs desugaring in typer.c (desugar_variadic_calls)
  only cloned NODE_IDENT arguments before passing to Array_set, not
  NODE_FIELD_ACCESS. When println(c.name) was desugared to
  Array_set(_va, 0, c.name), the builder emitted &c->name as the val
  argument. Array_set does free(val) after memcpy — since c.name is at
  offset 0, free(&c->name) freed the entire Container. Subsequent
  mallocs reused that memory, corrupting c.kind.

  Fix: added NODE_FIELD_ACCESS to the clone check in
  desugar_variadic_calls (typer.c:1357). Now field accesses are cloned
  before passing to Array_set, preventing the interior pointer free.

  Tests: src/test/enums.til test_payload_enum_field,
  test_payload_enum_field_b, test_payload_enum_field_default,
  test_payload_enum_field_mut.

* FIXED #52 Flaky parallel test failures
  Fixed by generalizing the test infrastructure: all tests (mode, FFI,
  typer, CLI, build) now go through a single blind parallel runner via
  test_runner.til subcommands (mode, single, count). Each test runs as
  an isolated subprocess with its own output file. The previous approach
  of running 4 modes × 32 files caused resource contention; the new
  runner handles scheduling with a slot-based pool (default j = thread count).

* FIXED #18 Tuple: dynamic heterogeneous collection
  A single dynamic Tuple struct in src/core/tuple.til with a raw data
  buffer and two parallel Vecs (type_names: Vec<Str>, type_sizes: Vec<U64>)
  for per-element type metadata. Follows the same patterns as Vec, Map,
  and Set.

  API: Tuple.new(), push(elem_type, elem_size, own val), get(i),
  type_at(i), size_at(i), clone(), delete(). Data buffer grows
  dynamically on push. Offset computation sums preceding element sizes.

  Tests in src/test/tuples.til: homogeneous I64, homogeneous Str,
  mixed types (I64+Str+Bool), type/size introspection, clone, growth.

  Tuple literal syntax ((10, 20), .0/.1 access, compile-time type
  annotations) are not implemented — those would be separate parser/typer
  work on top of this runtime struct.

* FIXED #49 Audit panic() call sites for correct __LOC__ usage
  All panic() calls now use __LOC__ as the first argument. Previously,
  core library files (str.til, vec.til, map.til, set.til, array.til) and
  self/til.til passed descriptive strings like "Str.get" or "Map.new" as
  the loc_str parameter instead of __LOC__. The context info was merged
  into the message string, e.g. panic(__LOC__, "Str.get: index out of bounds").

* FIXED #44 Integer literal coercion to non-I64 types
  Integer literals (0, 1, 42, etc.) always defaulted to I64, requiring
  explicit conversions like U64.from_i64(0) everywhere. The typer now
  coerces integer literals to the target type when unambiguous, with
  compile-time range validation for out-of-range values.

  *** Done
  - Typer coercion at 6 sites: typed declarations, namespace method call
    args, regular fcall args, assignments, field assignments, struct field
    defaults.
  - Compile-time range validation via literal_in_range() — rejects e.g.
    256 for U8, negative values for unsigned types.
  - Added I64.to_u8, I64.to_i16, I64.to_i32, I64.to_u32, I64.to_u64,
    I64.to_f32 ext_funcs for variable-to-type conversions (UFCS style).
  - Removed Type.from_i64() from all 6 type namespaces in core.til
    (U8, I16, I32, U32, U64, F32).
  - Cleaned up all call sites across the entire codebase (~200+ replacements):
    literal args → bare literals, variable args → val.to_TYPE(),
    inferred declarations → typed declarations.

* FIXED #48 Builder: hoist shallow args to stack values (50x regression fix)
  The builder heap-allocates every scalar temporary, even for operations on
  shallow parameters. Vec_push does 8 mallocs per call, Str_eq does ~40
  mallocs for a 10-byte comparison, every integer literal is heap-allocated.
  This caused the 50x regression when switching from hand-written C to
  generated core.c (a9fb721).

  The ~shallow~ keyword already marks pass-by-value parameters. The builder
  should emit stack values and direct C arithmetic for shallow types instead
  of malloc/free pairs. See doc/regression.org for full analysis.

  *** Done
  - 35df6c6: Fixed builder to handle ~returns shallow~ correctly:
    - Forward declarations (ext_func, namespace methods, top-level functions)
      use value return types when ~return_is_shallow~ is set.
    - ~emit_func_def~ checks flag for return type.
    - ~NODE_RETURN~ handles shallow-returning functions correctly.
    - Nested ext_funcs (e.g. ~U8_from_i64_ext~) registered in ~func_defs~ map.
    - Fixed C signature mismatches: ~U8_from_i64_ext~ return, ~U64_to_str_ext~ param.
  - 2578a19: Fixed remaining hardcoded return types in builder:
    - ~_size()~ functions respect ~return_is_shallow~.
    - Enum ~eq~ and ~is_Variant~ forward declarations respect flag.
    - ~dyn_has_method~ body emission respects flag.
  - 70d3a30: Added ~returns shallow~ to scalar ext_funcs in core.til:
    - 8 clone functions (U8, I16, I32, F32, U32, U64, I64, Bool).
    - 5 from_i64_ext functions (I16, I32, F32, U32, U64).
    - 5 system functions (dyn_has_method, check_cmd_status, file_mtime,
      clock_ms, get_thread_count).
    - Updated C implementations in ccore.c, ext.c, compat.c to return by value.
    - Fixed builder clone codegen in vec/array variadic helpers and struct
      field-assignment patterns.
  - 0f4e958: Hoisted scalar locals to stack values (the actual 50x fix):
    - Added ~is_scalar_type()~ helper and ~shallow_locals~ / ~unsafe_to_hoist~ Sets.
    - Pre-scan function bodies with ~collect_unsafe_to_hoist()~ to prevent hoisting
      variables passed to ~mut~ params or aliased by ~ref~ declarations.
    - ~emit_deref~ / ~emit_as_ptr~ check ~is_shallow_local()~ alongside ~is_shallow_param()~.
    - NODE_DECL emits ~Type x = value~ for hoisted scalars; unbox-and-free for non-shallow fcalls.
    - NODE_ASSIGN handles hoisted locals (direct assign vs pointer deref).
    - Delete calls for hoisted locals are suppressed (no heap allocation to free).
    - NODE_RETURN boxes hoisted locals when returning from non-shallow functions.
    - ~own~ args from hoisted locals malloc a copy (callee will ~free()~).
    - Hardcoded U64 temps in CLI variadic and vec_of_/array_of_ helpers converted to stack values.
    - Added ~-Wno-unused-but-set-variable~ to generated code compilation.
    - Result: compiled test time 52.76s → 22.41s (2.4x faster).

* FIXED #37 Improved mode lib: library compilation and FFI bindings
  Mode lib/liba/pure/pura for writing til libraries that produce C-compatible
  artifacts. Companion .c auto-detection replaced with explicit ~link_c()~
  directive. Auto-FFI via dlsym eliminates most handwritten dispatch wrappers.

*** Error gating
   ~interpret~ and ~run~ blocked for mode lib/liba with error message.
   ~test~ still works. Mode pure/pura not blocked (they auto-run tests).

*** translate (code generation)
   For mode lib/liba/pure/pura, translate generates three files:
   - ~gen/c/mylib.c~ — C implementation
   - ~gen/c/mylib.h~ — C header (structs, enums, function prototypes)
   - ~gen/til/mylib.til~ — FFI binding (~ext_struct~, ~ext_func~, ~ext_proc~,
     ~link("mylib")~)

*** build (compilation)
   Same as translate, plus compiles to:
   - ~gen/lib/libmylib.so~ — shared library
   - ~gen/lib/libmylib.a~ — static library

*** Scavenger skip
   Scavenger skipped for translate/build on lib/pure targets (all
   declarations are public API). Normal scavenging for test/run and
   when lib files are imported by other programs.

*** Auto-FFI via dlsym (dispatch.c wrapper elimination)
   ffi_init scans ext_func/ext_proc declarations, auto-discovers C
   functions via dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT). link() directives processed via
   dlopen for interpreter access.

   Eliminated ~60 handwritten h_* wrapper handlers from dispatch.c.
   Changed ccore.c/ext.c to value params + pointer returns, added
   ~shallow~ to all primitive arithmetic/comparison/conversion/inc/dec
   params in core.til. Auto-FFI (dlsym + libffi) handles them.

   Fixes: precomp ffi_init() for constant folding, VAL_PTR arg
   dereference for shallow dispatch, builder dyn_call void* casting.

   7 handlers remain (name mismatches): h_Bool_and/or/not,
   h_U8/I16/I32/U32_from_i64.

*** link_c() directive (replaces companion .c convention)
   Implicit companion .c auto-detection removed. Users now declare
   ~link_c("path/to/file.c")~ explicitly, parallel to ~link("libname")~.
   Path resolved relative to the .til file's directory.

   Commit 0b1ed97: ctil.c resolve_imports no longer searches for foo.c
   next to foo.til. c_files Vec removed from resolve_imports signature.

*** Shallow struct FFI (by-value struct params/returns)
   ~shallow~ keyword extended to support struct types in ext_func/ext_proc.
   Typer allows shallow on TIL_TYPE_STRUCT. dispatch.c shallow_ffi_type()
   builds ffi_type with FFI_TYPE_STRUCT and per-field elements array.
   Shallow struct params point arg_ptrs at StructInstance data buffer.
   Shallow struct returns allocate buffer, wrap in StructInstance.

   Builder emits ~StructName param~ (by value) instead of ~StructName *~.

   Commit e40d897 + fix in 42b3e04: Bool (ext_struct in core.til) was
   incorrectly matched as struct return — added cif.rtype FFI_TYPE_STRUCT
   check.

*** gui.c elimination
   Rewrote play_tone in gui.til using shallow struct FFI: Wave/Sound
   declared as ext_structs matching raylib's C layout, LoadSoundFromWave/
   PlaySound/UnloadSound called by value. gui.c deleted.

   Commit 6a5ae3f. Fix in 42b3e04: scavenger seeding, builder ref field
   assignment, Wave/Sound namespace + delete methods.

   Note: interpreter play_tone is slow (sample loop through eval dispatch
   vs native C). Documented in #24.

  Key commits: 940e369, 78956c1, 1a288e9, 0b1ed97, e40d897, 6a5ae3f,
  42b3e04.

  Install feature split to #42 (post-self-hosting).

* FIXED #2 ref keyword: non-owning references for returns, declarations, and struct fields
  ref returns: ~returns ref I64~ — caller does not own, ASAP skips.
  ref declarations: ~ref x := min(a, b)~ — binds without ownership.
  ASAP lifetime extension for args to ref-returning calls.
  ref struct fields: store pointer, clone copies pointer, delete skips.
  null literal: only valid in ref positions (ref locals, ref struct fields,
  borrowed params). Rejected for own/shallow params and non-ref locals.
  Args are already borrowed (ref) by default.

  Commit c3d3d81: null literal + ref struct fields.
  Earlier commits: ref returns, ref declarations.

  Note: ref tagged enum variants (ref payloads in enum) deferred — not
  needed for current use cases.

* FIXED #38 Drop til_ prefix from generated C symbols
  All ~til_~ prefix removed from builder, ext.c, dispatch.c, FFI C files.
  Generated C symbols now match the original C names (e.g. ~Token~ not
  ~til_Token~, ~malloc~ not ~til_malloc~).

  - builder.c: all ~til_~ prefix emission removed from types, functions,
    enums, structs, Vec, Array, dyn_call, dyn_has, CLI parsing, etc.
  - ext.h / ext.c: rewritten with unprefixed names
  - interpreter.c, dispatch.c: replaced ~til_~ type casts with bare types
  - ~func_to_c()~ helper maps C-colliding names: ~exit~ → ~exit_program~,
    ~sleep~ → ~sleep_ms~, ~double~ → ~double_~
  - Memory functions (malloc, calloc, realloc, memcpy, memmove): resolved
    via #23 shallow FFI — auto-FFI calls libc functions directly through
    libffi, no wrapper functions needed.

* FIXED #23 shallow keyword: direct C calling convention for ext_func/ext_proc
  Per-parameter ~shallow~ annotation on ext_func/ext_proc params tells the
  FFI to pass C values instead of til pointers.

  Example: ~malloc := ext_func(shallow count: I64) returns Dynamic {}~
  FFI passes int64_t by value, returns void* — no wrapper .c file needed.

  Implementation:
  - Parser: ~shallow~ keyword before param name in ext_func/ext_proc
  - AST: per-param ~param_shallows~ bool array on func_def
  - Typer: validates shallow only on scalar types (I64, U8, I16, I32, U32,
    Bool) and Dynamic
  - dispatch.c: dereferences values before FFI call for shallow params,
    uses ffi_type_sint64/uint8/etc. instead of ffi_type_pointer
  - builder.c: emits direct C types for shallow params
  - Auto-FFI: ffi_init scans all ext_func/ext_proc declarations, uses
    ~dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT, name)~ to find C library functions without
    needing a companion .c file. Silently skips functions not found
    (handled by dispatch_map). Processes ~link("lib")~ directives via
    ~dlopen("libNAME.so", RTLD_NOW | RTLD_GLOBAL)~.
  - Dynamic return type: FFI dispatch wraps void* returns as VAL_PTR
  - Companion .c files eliminated for standard C library calls (malloc,
    calloc, realloc, memcpy, memmove now go through auto-FFI)

* FIXED #40 Func returning borrowed param: use-after-free in both interpret and run
  When a func returns a borrowed parameter (~return a~ where ~a~ is not
  ~own~), the returned pointer IS the same pointer as the caller's argument.
  The typer's ASAP system hoists literal args to temps and deletes them
  after the call, freeing the return value's memory.

  Root cause: the typer hoists ~max_val(5, 3)~ into ~_t1 := 5; _t2 := 3;
  r := max_val(_t1, _t2); I64.delete(_t1); I64.delete(_t2)~. Since
  ~max_val~ returns borrowed ~a~ directly, ~r~ and ~_t1~ are the same
  pointer — deleting ~_t1~ frees ~r~'s data (use-after-free in interpret,
  double-free crash in run).

  Affects both interpreter and builder because both evaluate the typer's
  ASAP-inserted delete calls. Not mode-specific — appeared in cli mode
  because that's where the pattern was first used.

  Fix: auto-insert clone in the typer when a non-ref-returning function
  returns a borrowed parameter (~return <ident>~ where ident is a non-own
  param). Added ~returns_ref~ parameter to ~infer_body~ so ref-returning
  functions (which intentionally return the same pointer) are not cloned.

* FIXED #41 Typer: ASAP delete before reassignment causes use-after-free
  ~x = x.concat(y)~ generated ~Type_delete(x)~ before the assignment,
  but ~concat~ still needed to read ~x~. Fixed by checking
  ~expr_uses_var(rhs, vname)~ — when true, the typer sets
  ~save_old_delete~ on the NODE_ASSIGN and the builder emits:
  ~{ Type *_old = x; x = RHS; Type_delete(_old, ...); }~

* FIXED #31 Variadic argument forwarding (splat)
  ~..expr~ at call sites spreads an Array into variadic arguments.
  Parser detects ~TOK_DOTDOT~ before args, sets ~is_splat~ flag on AST node.
  Typer's ~desugar_variadic_calls~ handles pure splat by passing the Array
  directly (cloned if ident) instead of constructing a new Array.
  Enables forwarding variadic args: ~func(..nums)~ and splatting arrays.

* FIXED #35 Switch statement (phases 1-4)
  Syntax: ~switch expr { case val: body  case: body }~. Implicit break, no
  fall-through. Default case is ~case:~. Desugars in typer to if/else chains.

  Phase 1: Simple value matching — ~.eq()~ comparison for I64, U8, Str, Bool,
  and enums. NODE_SWITCH/NODE_CASE AST nodes, parser, typer desugaring.

  Phase 2: Range cases — ~case 'a'..'z':~ detects Range.new() pattern from
  parser's ~..~ desugaring, emits ~_sw.gte(A).and(_sw.lte(B))~ (inclusive).

  Phase 3: Enum variant matching — free via ~.eq()~ from Phase 1.

  Phase 4: Payload extraction — ~case Token.Num(value):~ desugars to
  ~_sw.is_Num()~ + ~value := _sw.get_Num()~. Typer detects enum variant
  constructor pattern and generates is/get calls with binding.

  Phase 5 (nested patterns) split to #36, depends on #12 (self-hosting).

* FIXED #33 Character literals for U8
  Single-quoted character syntax: ~'a'~, ~'('~, ~'\n'~. Type is always U8.
  Escape sequences: ~'\n'~ ~'\t'~ ~'\r'~ ~'\\'~ ~'\''~ ~'\0'~.

  No new AST node — char literals desugar to NODE_LITERAL_NUM with TIL_TYPE_U8
  at parse time. Lexer stores raw content between quotes (TOK_CHAR), parser
  resolves escape sequences to byte value and creates numeric literal.
  Interpreter and builder unchanged (already handle U8 numeric literals).

  Self-hosted lexer.til simplified: removed 34 CH_* constants, replaced all
  ~U8.from_i64(N)~ with inline char literals. Much closer to lexer.c now.

* FIXED #34 For-in loops over collections
  Syntax: ~for i in collection { body }~ or ~for i : Type in collection { body }~.
  Works for any type with ~get(I64)~ and ~len()~ methods (Vec, Array, Str, Range).
  Type inference: if ~get()~ returns concrete type, annotation optional; if
  ~ref Dynamic~ (Vec, Array), explicit type required.

  Desugars in the typer to a while loop inside an anonymous scope. Index
  increment placed before user body so ~continue~ doesn't cause infinite loop.
  Typer validates iterable interface (len/get signatures).

  Range iteration: ~for i in 0..10 { body }~ — parser desugars ~0..10~ to
  ~Range.new(0, 10)~. Range struct in core.til with start/end I64 fields.
  Ascending and descending ranges supported. ~get()~ returns I64 directly
  (not ref), for-in handles both ref and non-ref get() returns.

  Also added ~inc()~/~dec()~ ext_func to all integer types (I64, U8, I16,
  I32, U32) as prerequisite for future ~++~/~--~ sugar (#22).

  Tests: src/test/loops.til (16 tests), 3 typer error cases.

* FIXED #29 Proper strings: separate count/cap, static literals, views
  See doc/strings.org for full design.

  Str has ~count~ (content length) and ~cap~ (allocated capacity or sentinel).
  Named constants ~CAP_LIT~ (-1) and ~CAP_VIEW~ (-2) in str.til encode ownership.

** DONE Phase 1: Add count field (structural, count == cap everywhere)
   Str struct has ~mut count := 0~ and ~mut cap := 0~.

** DONE Phase 2: Static string literals (CAP_LIT)
   Builder emits string literal data inline with cap=TIL_CAP_LIT (-1).
   Interpreter also uses cap=-1 for literals.
   Str.delete skips free(data) when cap < 0.

** DONE Phase 3: String views (CAP_VIEW)
   ~substr~ returns zero-copy view with cap=CAP_VIEW (-2). Data points into
   source buffer, no malloc/memcpy. Call ~.clone()~ to materialize ownership.
   Dangling pointer risk accepted (no borrow checker).
   ~push_str~ panics on views/literals (cap < 0) — clone first to mutate.
   ~split()~ clones substr results before storing in Vec.
   Include chain fix: ext.h → aliases.h, builder drops ccore.h from generated
   includes. Test mode initializes top-level core variables in main().
   Builder: in_func_def flag prevents script globals shadowing function locals.
   Builder: no-mode files default to script mode (is_script = !mode || !mode->decls_only).
   C Str (str.c) mirrors view changes: substr returns view, delete checks cap,
   push_str panics on cap < 0, to_i64 uses stack buffer for non-null-terminated views.
   Interpreter string literals use cap=-1 (CAP_LIT) so views into them don't dangle.

** DONE Phase 4: In-place mutation (with_capacity, push_str)
   ~Str.with_capacity(n)~ creates Str with count=0, cap=n.
   ~Str.push_str(s)~ appends in-place, panics if capacity exceeded (no realloc —
   buffers never move so views stay valid).
   format() rewritten to use ~with_capacity~ + ~push_str~.

** FIXED #46 Optimize Str with with_capacity in concat, join, etc.
   The remaining semantic and optimization work around ~Str.with_capacity~
   is now done.

   What landed:
   - fixed the interpreter bug where struct construction was moving every
     identifier arg unconditionally instead of obeying ~is_own_arg~
     - that was consuming globals like ~CAP_VIEW~ through
       ~Str(..., cap = CAP_VIEW)~ in ~substr~
     - this was the real cause of the old interp-only ~with_capacity/push_str~
       regression
   - restored the disabled ~strings.til~ coverage for the
     ~with_capacity + push_str~ path
   - switched the hot string builders to the intended
     ~with_capacity~ design directly:
     - ~format~
     - ~concat~
     - ~replace~
     - ~join~
   - updated ~strings.org~ to match the real ~Str~ fields and current
     optimized-builder design

* FIXED #1 builder/interpreter consistency: eliminate unnecessary special cases
** FIXED #85 C code must use enum structs, not raw tags
   This one is effectively done as part of the #39 bottom-up migration.

   The original blocker was the old handwritten-C parser/AST surface using
   raw enum-tag APIs like ~check(..., TokenType_TAG_Ident)~ instead of the
   generated enum-struct form. That parser-era pattern is now gone from
   ~src/c~.

   What still exists in handwritten C is normal generated-layout use:
   - reading ~.tag~ on enum structs
   - switching on generated tag enums
   - constructing full enum values/structs where needed

   That is not the old #85 problem. There are also no handwritten
   ~typedef enum { ... }~ definitions left in ~src/c~; enum type layout is
   coming from til/generated headers now.

   So #85 is considered fixed by the self-hosting work:
   - the old raw-tag C API surface is gone
   - remaining C enum use matches the generated representation

** FIXED #119 Struct literals take ownership except for ref fields
   Struct literals now follow one explicit rule:

   - ~ref~ fields are borrowed
   - every other field is ownership-taking

   This means struct literals are no longer copy sites. Fresh values
   such as literals, nested constructors, and defaults still need no
   clone, but passing a variable into a non-~ref~ field now consumes it
   and lets the normal use-after-own checks suggest ~.clone()~ when the
   caller wants to keep using the source.

   What landed:
   - typer marks constructor args as ownership-taking for ownership-relevant
     non-~ref~ fields
   - scalar value fields no longer trigger bogus ownership transfer
   - builder ctor emission was simplified so it stops inventing clone policy
     for inline fields
   - parser/AST helper constructors were adjusted to clone borrowed inputs
     before storing them into owned fields
   - the normal suite is green again under this model

   Follow-ups left separate:
   - ~#46~ still tracks the interpreter-specific ~with_capacity~ regression
   - ~#99~ still tracks later constructor/new-surface unification

  Two changes to eliminate backend special cases:

  1. Enum eq generated as real til in initer (was ext_func with C in both
     backends). Uses is_Variant/get_Variant (which stay ext_func) to build
     if-chain. Also extended is_Variant to simple enums (was payload-only).
     Removed ~160 lines from builder, ~49 lines from dispatch.

  2. println/print/format replaced with print_single/print_flush pattern.
     print_single(Str) and print_flush() are simple ext_procs. println/print
     are real til procs looping over variadic Array. format is real til func
     using Str.concat loop. Removed special-case call emission, C
     implementation emission, and interpreter handlers (~90 lines total).

  3. Interpreter shallow copies replaced with deep clones to match builder.
     The interpreter used raw memcpy in write_field, NODE_FIELD_ASSIGN, and
     value_to_buf for struct values, sharing internal heap pointers (e.g.
     Str's c_str) between what should be independent copies. Fixed all three
     sites to check val.instance->borrowed and call clone_value for borrowed
     values instead of memcpy. Also made clone_value properly recursive:
     own fields now recursively call clone_value (was only one level deep
     with strndup for Str), and inline struct fields (e.g. Vec inside
     FuncDefData) are now recursively deep-cloned (were previously left as
     shallow memcpy). This fixed 3 interpreter-only test failures (structs,
     splat, parsing — all use-after-free crashes from shared c_str pointers).

  Remaining differences (dyn_call, primitive types, struct constructors,
  ownership tracking, namespace init) are inherent to the execution models
  and marked as acceptable.

* FIXED #15 Scavenger doesn't understand dyn_call/dyn_has_method/array/vec
  Collections (Vec, Set, Map, Array) call methods dynamically via dyn_call
  with string literal method names, invisible to the scavenger's static
  analysis. This caused size() and cmp() to be removed as dead code.

  Fix: always keep delete, clone, size, and cmp for every struct/enum that
  survives scavenging. These are the infrastructure methods used by
  collections via dyn_call. If a method doesn't exist on a type, the BFS
  visit is harmless (no entry in the methods map).

  Also simplified the array()/vec() special cases in collect_refs — removed
  redundant Type.size() extraction and type name refs (now covered by the
  always-keep logic and ext_func return type).

  File: src/c/scavenger.c

* FIXED #30 Vec-in-struct: mutating an inline struct field operates on a copy
  Accessing a struct field of compound type (Vec, Point, Str, any user-defined
  struct) returned a copy instead of a reference. Mutations to the copy were
  discarded. E.g. c.items.push(own 10) pushed into a clone, original unchanged.

** Root causes (three bugs)
   1. interpreter.c read_field(): copied inline struct fields when parent was
      not borrowed, instead of always returning a borrowed reference into the
      parent buffer (which is what codegen does with &(c->items)).
   2. initer.c compute_struct_layout(): didn't set field_struct_def for fields
      with namespace constructor defaults (e.g. Vec.new(...)) because the FCALL
      callee detection only handled NODE_IDENT callees (Point()), not
      NODE_FIELD_ACCESS callees (Vec.new()). Without field_struct_def,
      read_field fell through to the I64 fallback.
   3. typer.c: clone insertion for declarations/assignments only triggered for
      NODE_IDENT, not NODE_FIELD_ACCESS. After fixing read_field to always
      borrow, x := c.items needed a clone to get an independent copy.

** Fix
   - interpreter.c: read_field() always returns borrowed for inline struct
     fields (sub->data = ptr, sub->borrowed = 1).
   - initer.c: detect namespace FCALL defaults (Vec.new(...)) by checking
     for NODE_FIELD_ACCESS callee and extracting struct name from its child.
   - typer.c: extend 3 clone insertion sites to also clone NODE_FIELD_ACCESS
     of struct/enum type (not primitives — their struct_name holds the parent
     struct name, not the field's own type).

* FIXED #19 Literal syntax for collection initialization
  Sugar for initializing Array, Vec, Set, Map, and Tuple with values
  instead of repeated .set()/.push()/.add() calls.

** Phase 1: array() and vec() builtins
   arr := array("I64", 1, 2, 3)
   v := vec("I64", 10, 20, 30)

   Implemented as ext_func builtins hardcoded in typer.c (validate first
   arg is string literal), dispatch.c (C handlers), and builder.c (emit
   as til_array_of_I64(3, v1, v2, v3)). Requires C-side code in all
   three places.

** Phase 2: Set and Map literals
   m := {"a": 1, "b": 2}      // Map[Str, I64]
   s := {1, 2, 3}              // Set[I64]

   Different approach from array/vec: desugars to pure til code in the
   typer, so NO new dispatch.c handlers or builder.c special cases.

   Parser: {expr: expr, ...} → NODE_MAP_LIT, {expr, expr, ...} →
   NODE_SET_LIT. Disambiguation by peeking for ':' after first expr.

   Typer: NODE_MAP_LIT/NODE_SET_LIT inferred in infer_expr (sets
   til_type=TIL_TYPE_STRUCT, struct_name="Map"/"Set"). Post-pass
   functions desugar_map_literals() and desugar_set_literals() replace
   the literal with:
   - Map: mut m := Map.new(key_type, key_size, val_type, val_size)
          + Map.set(m, own k, own v) per entry
   - Set: mut s := Set.new(elem_type, elem_size)
          + Set.add(s, own v) per element

   Compile-time validation (shared by interpreter and codegen):
   - All keys/elements same type, all values same type
   - Key/element type must implement cmp
   Any type with cmp works as key: I64, Str, Bool, user structs with cmp.

** Phase 3: Tuple literals
   t := (1, "hello", true)   // Tuple[I64, Str, Bool]

   Deferred to #18 (Tuple type implementation).

* FIXED #27 Test functions and mode test
  New function kind: test (FUNC_TEST). Like proc but auto-run by
  runtime, cannot be called manually, root-scope only, no params,
  no return type. Typer enforces all constraints.

  mode test: decls_only=yes, needs_main=no. Runtime auto-runs all
  test functions in declaration order, reports pass/fail.

  bin/ctil test: universal test execution on any file regardless of
  mode. Ignores main/top-level statements, runs only test functions.

  Renamed core.til `test` proc to `expect` to free the keyword.

  Limitations of mode test (decls_only):
  - Top-level non-decl statements are not executed in codegen mode
    (scavenger may also remove them). Move workaround refs into tests.
  - Top-level constant initializers (N := 10) are not run in codegen,
    causing NULL dereferences. Move constants inside test functions.
  - Imported top-level constants have the same issue. Tests that depend
    on imported constants (e.g. import_diamond.til) must stay mode script.

  Files kept as mode script/cli for specific reasons:
  - typer_errors.til: deliberately triggers type errors, not runnable
  - globals.til: tests script-mode global variable behavior
  - globals_cli.til: tests CLI-mode globals
  - import_diamond.til: uses imported top-level constant (SHARED_VAL)
  - lexing.til: large file (500+ lines) with full tokenizer impl
  - parsing.til: large file (1000+ lines) with full parser impl

* FIXED #26 Mode struct: eliminate string-based mode checks
  Replaced ad-hoc strcmp/Str_eq_c mode checks across 5 files with a
  Mode struct in modes.h with properties: name, needs_main, decls_only,
  auto_import. All backends receive const Mode* instead of Str*.

  Adding a new mode now means one struct definition in modes.h instead
  of touching every backend file. auto_import field generalizes the
  gui.til/gui.c loading pattern to any mode.

* FIXED #16 Imports: multi-file programs
  import "path.til" syntax loads declarations from other files into the
  program scope. Flat include with dedup (realpath + Set), cycle detection
  with clear error chain, companion .c file accumulation for FFI.

  Path resolution: relative to importing file, then src/lib/ (standard
  library). Imported files cannot declare mode. Zero downstream changes
  — init, typer, precomp, scavenger, codegen, interpreter all untouched.

  Tests: src/test/imports.til, src/test/import_diamond.til (+ helpers).

* FIXED #11 Mode GUI: raylib integration via FFI
  `mode gui` auto-provides raylib FFI bindings. Users write only app logic
  with a main proc — no link() directives, ext_func declarations, or
  companion .c files needed.

  Implementation: `src/modes/gui.til` (raylib declarations + link directives)
  and `src/modes/gui.c` (C wrappers bridging til pointer convention to raylib
  value types) are auto-loaded by ctil.c when mode is "gui".

  gui.til AST is merged after core.til but before user code. Declarations are
  NOT marked is_core so they go through normal FFI paths (dlsym in interpreter,
  forward declarations in codegen). The existing link() extraction handles
  gui.til's link directives automatically.

  Mode "gui" behaves like "cli" for entry point: requires a main proc,
  scavenger seeds from "main", codegen generates C main(). The scavenger
  prunes unused raylib functions.

  gui.c is set as the companion .c file. If the user also has their own
  companion .c, both are compiled together (space-separated in cc command).

  Files: src/modes/gui.til, src/modes/gui.c, src/examples/gui_hello.til.
  Works in both `bin/ctil run` and `bin/ctil interpret`.

* FIXED #8 Variadic args for funcs, procs, and macros
  `..Type` variadic parameters for all function types: regular func/proc,
  macros, ext_func/ext_proc, and main CLI args.

** Phase 1: General variadic args for user-defined functions
   Variadic args stored as til Array. Typer desugars call sites into
   Array.new + Array.set + single Array arg. Both backends see normal code.
   Parser rejects own/mut on `..T` and positional params after variadic.

** Phase 2: main proc CLI args (including variadic)
   main := proc(args: ..Str) works in both compiled and interpreted modes.
   CLI args validated and parsed at runtime (Str, I64, U8, Bool types).

** Phase 3: Variadic args in FFI functions
   Typer desugaring now applies to non-core ext_func/ext_proc. C side
   receives til_Array* and iterates via til_Array_elem() helper from ext.h.
   Fixes: typer guard changed to `!is_builtin || !func_def->is_core`,
   is_core propagated to func_def children, Array typedef/forward-decl
   skipped in codegen (provided by ext.h).
   Tested with sum_all(nums: ..I64) in ext_module.til/ext_module.c.

* FIXED #4 FFI: call external C functions from til
  Users can call C functions by providing a .c file alongside their .til program.
  Declare signatures in til with ext_func/ext_proc, implement in C with til_
  prefix and pointer calling convention.

  Codegen path: builder.c emits forward declarations, compile_c links user .c.
  Non-core ext_structs get typedefs and forward declarations.

  Interpreter path: dispatch.c compiles user .c to .so, dlopen/dlsym into
  FFIEntry map. libffi-based calling (no arity cap). ffi_cif precomputed per
  function. Flat struct representation (StructInstance.data is C-layout-compatible)
  passed directly as args and wrapped on return (including ext_struct types).

  CLI -l flag support: ctil parses -l flags from argv, forwards to both
  compile_c and ffi_init cc commands. Enables linking external libraries
  (e.g. -l raylib -l GL -l m).

  Companion .c file detection: ctil.c checks for program.c next to program.til.
  Limited but functional. Proper mechanism (command-line flag, pragma) deferred.

  Remaining limitation: til's pointer calling convention (til_I64 *, til_Str *)
  doesn't match natural C conventions (int, const char *). External libraries
  still require wrapper .c files. See #23 (shallow keyword) for the fix.

  Tests: src/examples/ffi/ext_module.til (both interpret and codegen).
  Demo: src/examples/raylib.til + raylib.c (raylib via FFI, both modes).

* FIXED #20 Dynamic arg narrowing
  Dynamic values can be passed where a concrete type is expected.
  The parameter's declared type provides context, like an explicit
  type annotation on a declaration.
  - 34.add(vec.get(0)) works (param type narrows Dynamic arg)
  - 0.add(v.get(0)).add(v.get(1)) works (typed start, chain continues)
  - bv.get(0).and(bv.get(1)) works (top-level UFCS, unique func name)
  - vec.get(0).add(35) errors with suggestion to use ref declaration
    (namespace UFCS on Dynamic is genuinely ambiguous)
  Also added arg type checking (was missing entirely):
  32.add(true) now errors instead of producing garbage.

* FIXED #21 Universal UFCS: method syntax for top-level functions
  a.f(b) now falls back to top-level f(a, b) when f is not found in
  a's type namespace. The typer checks the top-level scope for a
  func/proc whose first parameter type matches a's type. If found,
  rewrites the AST from a field_access call to a plain function call.
  Namespace methods still take priority.

  Single change in typer.c (~line 178): added fallback before the
  "no method" error. Uses goto to jump to the regular call path after
  rewriting. No changes needed in interpreter or codegen.

  Enables: "hello".println(), 5.double(), x.transform().validate()
  where these are all top-level functions.

* FIXED #5 Pointers, Vec, Array, native Str: C-level memory and dynamic structures
  :DEPENDENCIES: #2 #3
  Evolved from 4 pointer primitives into a full inline-value memory model.
  Dynamic structs (Vec, Array) store values inline in raw buffers via memcpy.
  Str became a native til struct (was ext_struct with hidden fields and C
  methods). Auto-generated clone/delete/size work for all types, including
  structs with own fields pointing to raw buffers.

** Phase 1: Pointer primitives and Vec

*** FIXED Pointer primitives (ext_func)
    Added to core.til, implemented in ext.c, dispatch.c handles interpreter:

    malloc := ext_func(count: I64) returns Dynamic {}
    realloc := ext_func(own buf: Dynamic, count: I64) returns Dynamic {}
    ptr_add := ext_func(buf: Dynamic, offset: I64) returns ref Dynamic {}
    memcpy := ext_proc(dest: Dynamic, src: Dynamic, len: I64) {}
    free := ext_proc(own val: Dynamic) {}

    Inline value model: memcpy copies raw bytes into buffers, ptr_add returns
    a pointer into the buffer (pure arithmetic, no indirection). Values live
    directly in buffers — no shallow pointer copies.

    Interpreter: VAL_PTR holds raw void* (same as codegen). h_malloc returns
    calloc, h_ptr_add does (char*)buf + offset, h_memcpy calls C memcpy,
    h_free calls C free. val_to_ptr() helper extracts void* from any Value.

*** FIXED Dynamic as a real type
    builder: TIL_TYPE_DYNAMIC → "void *". emit_as_ptr/emit_deref skip
    indirection for Dynamic. typer: numeric 0 accepted for Dynamic (null).

*** FIXED Parser: ref with explicit type annotation
    ref name : Type = expr (explicit type for Dynamic reinterpretation).

*** FIXED Type.size() auto-generation
    Computed from fields in initer.c: own/pointer fields = 8 bytes, inline
    primitives = their size, inline structs = recursive sum. Str.size() = 16
    (8-byte pointer + 8-byte I64). Vec.size() = 48 (6 fields × 8).

*** FIXED Vec and Array structs in core.til
    Vec: growable dynamic array. Array: fixed-size with typed element storage.
    Both use memcpy+free to move values inline into raw buffers.
    dyn_call1/dyn_call2/dyn_call1_ret/dyn_call2_ret for type-erased method
    dispatch (clone, delete) on buffer elements.

    Vec.push: memcpy(dest, val, elem_size); free(own val)
    Vec.get: returns ptr_add(data, i * elem_size) as ref Dynamic
    Vec.delete: dyn_call2(elem_type, "delete", ptr_add(...), false) per element
    Vec.clone: dyn_call1_ret(elem_type, "clone", ...) + memcpy per element

*** FIXED dyn_call dispatch (4 variants)
    dyn_call1, dyn_call2, dyn_call1_ret, dyn_call2_ret — ext_func wrappers
    for type-erased method calls. Interpreter builds synthetic AST call nodes.
    Codegen emits static dispatch functions with if-chains matching type name
    strings (memcmp on til_Str fields).

** Phase 2: Native Str (was ext_struct)

*** FIXED Str as native struct in core.til
    Replaced ext_struct (hidden fields, all C methods) with a regular struct:

    Str := struct {
        own data : U8 = 0
        cap := 0
        namespace:
        new, len, char_at, eq, concat, clone, delete, to_str,
        substr, contains, starts_with, ends_with
    }

    All methods implemented in til using ptr_add, memcpy, malloc, free.
    String literals remain special syntax that constructs Str instances.

*** FIXED I64.to_str and U8.to_str in til
    Replaced C ext_funcs with pure til implementations. I64.to_str builds
    digit string via repeated div/mod, allocates buffer, fills with memcpy.

*** FIXED Removed TIL_TYPE_STR from type system
    Str is now TIL_TYPE_STRUCT with struct_name="Str" everywhere:
    ast.h (removed enum value), typer.c, initer.c, precomp.c, builder.c.

*** FIXED Interpreter: VAL_STR kept as runtime representation
    Str values use VAL_STR wrapping compiler-internal Str struct (same
    pointer+cap layout). Str() constructor intercepts struct instantiation
    to create VAL_STR from data (VAL_PTR/VAL_U8) + cap args. Field access
    on VAL_STR: .data → VAL_PTR, .cap → VAL_I64.

    VAL_PTR → VAL_STR reinterpretation at function call boundaries: when
    dyn_call passes ptr_add result to Str.delete/clone, the VAL_PTR is
    reinterpreted as VAL_STR based on param type annotation. Handles both
    ident args (borrowed cells) and expression args.

*** FIXED Codegen: til_Str typedef in ext.h
    til_Str { til_U8 *data; til_I64 cap } defined in ext.h so FFI code
    can use it. Generated C file skips emitting Str typedef (ext.h provides).
    String literals emit til_Str_lit("...", cap) helper. println/print/format
    emit static helpers with __attribute__((unused)).

*** FIXED Codegen: forward declaration ordering
    Split emit_struct_def into emit_struct_typedef + emit_struct_funcs.
    Generated C file order: all struct typedefs → string helpers → all
    function bodies (struct namespace + enum + top-level) → dyn_call
    dispatch → main. Prevents incomplete-type errors from cross-struct
    references.

*** FIXED Typer: own fields skip auto-clone in constructors
    Str(data = buf, cap = n) — the data field is own, so the typer must
    NOT insert clone (which would copy 1 byte instead of moving the whole
    buffer pointer). Constructor args for own fields are marked is_own_arg
    and use move semantics.

*** FIXED FFI updated for til_Str
    greet.c uses til_Str * (includes ext.h) instead of compiler-internal
    Str *. Ext_func forward declarations in generated code use
    type_name_to_c() for proper til_ prefixed types.

** Preparation renames (commit 80263df)
   alloc→malloc, Str.len→cap, Vec.len→count, Array.len→cap,
   len() methods return cap/count fields. size() computed from fields.

** Files modified (cumulative)
   | File               | Action                                            |
   | src/core/core.til  | Str native struct, I64/U8.to_str in til, Vec/Array use memcpy |
   | src/c/ext.h        | til_Str typedef, updated declarations             |
   | src/c/ext.c        | Removed Str/I64_to_str/U8_to_str funcs, added memcpy/ptr_add |
   | src/c/interpreter.h | Raw void* in Value union (PtrInst removed)       |
   | src/c/interpreter.c | Str() constructor, VAL_STR field access, PTR→STR reinterpret |
   | src/c/dispatch.c   | Raw pointer handlers, dyn_call, removed Str handlers |
   | src/c/typer.c      | TIL_TYPE_STR→STRUCT, own field clone skip         |
   | src/c/builder.c   | Split struct typedef/funcs, til_Str_lit, forward decl ordering |
   | src/c/ast.h        | Removed TIL_TYPE_STR                              |
   | src/c/initer.c     | Str as regular struct, size() from fields          |
   | src/c/precomp.c    | Str as TIL_TYPE_STRUCT                            |
   | src/examples/ffi/greet.c | Updated to use til_Str                      |
   | src/test/vecs.til  | Updated size expectations                         |
   | tests.sh           | Added arrays.til to test list                     |


* FIXED #9 Tagged unions (enums): enum definitions with optional payloads
  til lacks enums and unions. Tagged unions (like Rust enums) combine both:
  variants are named alternatives, optionally carrying a payload. Essential
  for translating C code to til (C uses enums + unions extensively).

  Syntax follows struct conventions — EnumDef type annotation is optional
  (inferred from enum keyword). Trailing commas allowed.

    Color := enum { Red, Green, Blue }

    Token := enum {
        Num: I64,
        Name: Str,
        Eof,
    }

  Variants are accessed as namespace members: Color.Red, Color.Green.
  Payload variants are constructed as calls: Token.Num(42).
  Payload extraction via auto-generated is_Variant / get_Variant methods
  (no match statement yet).

  Enums have a namespace section just like structs, separated by
  namespace:. The initer auto-generates clone, delete, eq, and to_str
  if missing, same as structs.

** FIXED Phase 1: simple enums (no payloads)
   Variants are named integer constants (0, 1, 2, ...). Internally stored
